inline_comments.message: Will this work if the "=" character is in the middle of the password? Or is it a leading "=" that we are removing?

It should work preserving a password that contains a "=" in it in a property in a properties file, see for example:

[09:59|~] echo "javax.persistence.jdbc.password=mypassword=" | awk -F= '$1 == "javax.persistence.jdbc.password" { st = index($0,"="); print substr($0,st+1) }'
mypassword=
[09:59|~] echo "javax.persistence.jdbc.password=mypass=word" | awk -F= '$1 == "javax.persistence.jdbc.password" { st = index($0,"="); print substr($0,st+1) }'
mypass=word
[09:59|~] echo "javax.persistence.jdbc.password==mypassword" | awk -F= '$1 == "javax.persistence.jdbc.password" { st = index($0,"="); print substr($0,st+1) }'
=mypassword
[09:59|~] 

before the password was truncated at the "=" sign so the credentials were invalid.
